terminal_a.role: SASA zinc capping + CRO prep + patent drafting (CPU only)
terminal_a.notes: All CPU tasks complete. SASA, contact maps, CRO specs, patent draft done. Compiler ready. Waiting for MD results from B/C/D.

terminal_c.role: MD controls vs ERAP2-K392 + IRAP â€” RUNS FASGAV GATE FIRST (GPU)

notes: FASGAV gate: RMSD stays <2A (rigid 6-mer doesnt deform) but COM drift = 6.6A at 5.6ns. Peptide is sliding out as rigid body. Gate PASSES on COM drift metric. For rigid 6-mers, COM drift is better than RMSD for detecting ejection. FASGAV running to 10ns for full picture, then C starts remaining 3 runs.

terminal_e.role: V2 protein binder channel-capping redesign (CPU + GPU)

phase2_boltz2.root_cause: All modifications contact conserved residues, destroying selectivity

terminal_e.conclusion: V2 binder channel-capping redesign does NOT work with simple loop extensions or peptide grafts. The modifications contact conserved residues shared across ERAP1/ERAP2/IRAP. The original V2 binder selectivity (0.748 ERAP2, 0.186 ERAP1) came from targeting ONLY the divergent patch. Recommend: (1) Keep original V2 as selective surface binder, (2) Pursue V4 short-stack peptides independently for channel inhibition, (3) Do NOT combine V2+V4 — the hybrid loses both selectivity and binding strength.
